UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. AND RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BANK OF AMERICA FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE TENDER AND/OR EXEMPTION FROM TENDER REQUIREMENT AS TO ALLEGEDLY DISPUTED PORTION OF THE DEBT; AND ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT BANK OF AMERICA IS DEBT COLLECTOR. SUSTAIN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO RECONTRUST, NO OPPOSITION. PREVAILING PARTY PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE A. JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 12, 2013. MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED AGAIN (SEE CGC-13-529041) TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.6C AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPY OF SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. OPPOSING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.6B AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF OPPOSITION. ALL PAPERS MUST BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 501 NO LATER THAN JULY 26, 2013 WITH COVER LETTER REFLECTING NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE TENDER AND DUTY OF CARE. ONOFRIO, DIMOCK AND ANSANELLI ARE DISTINGUISHABLE. OVERRULED AS TO THE DEBT COLLECTION ACTS - COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT IS A DEBT COLLECTOR BY ITS OWN ADMISSION. SEE EX 2 TO SAC. DEFENDANT HAS 30 DAYS TO ANSWER. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Continued To Dec-18-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Grace B. Kang, Esq. of McGuire Woods, (310-315-8200), APPEARING THROUGH COURT CALL, for Bank of America, Defendant and moving party
Kenley Dygert, Esq. of Consumer Action Law Group, (818-254-8413), APPEARING THROUGH COURT CALL, for John Armor, Plaintiff and opposing party

Brief discussion re the tentative ruling is held. Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action for negligence, Plaintiff failed to allege tender and duty of care. Onofrio, Dimock and Ansanelli are distinguishable. OVERRULED as to the debt collection acts - Complaint sufficiently alleges that Defendant is a debt collector by its own admission. See Ex 2 to SAC. Defendant has 30 days to answer. Moving party to submit order approved as to form. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
